info.title: API Extractor
info.description: Brazilian economic indicators extraction, storage, and dashboard API. 25 macro indicators from BCB SGS, IBGE SIDRA, and B3/Yahoo Finance. Supports on-demand transforms, pinned dashboards, and release calendars.

get.description: Returns the overall health status and a list of all 25 series with their current freshness state (fresh | stale | failed) and the timestamp of the last successful extraction. The frontend sidebar uses this to display the sync indicator.

Returns all 25 configured series with their metadata and current extraction status. Optionally filter by category (e.g. 'Inflação', 'Juros', 'Câmbio', 'Mercado', 'Atividade', 'Trabalho', 'Fiscal', 'Externo').

parameters.name: category

schema.description: Filter by category name (case-sensitive, pt-BR). Omit to return all.
schema.title: Category

parameters.description: Filter by category name (case-sensitive, pt-BR). Omit to return all.

get.description: Returns full metadata for one economic indicator series, including source information, frequency, unit, and current extraction status. Used by the Metadados dossier page.

get.summary: Get raw observations for a series
get.description: Returns stored time-series observations for the given series. Supports optional date range filtering via `from` and `to` (ISO-8601). Results are ordered by observed_at ascending. Maximum 5000 rows returned per request (default 500). Decimal values stored in Postgres are returned as float64.

parameters.name: from

schema.description: Start date (inclusive). ISO-8601: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.
schema.title: From

parameters.description: Start date (inclusive). ISO-8601: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.

parameters.name: to

schema.description: End date (inclusive). ISO-8601: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.
schema.title: To

parameters.description: End date (inclusive). ISO-8601: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum rows to return. Default 500, max 5000.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum rows to return. Default 500, max 5000.

422.description: Invalid date format or limit out of range.

post.summary: Apply a transform to a series
post.description: Applies the requested statistical transform to all stored observations for the series. Results are cached in Redis (TTL: daily=1h, monthly=24h, quarterly=7d). Cache key includes the transform spec hash and the latest observed_at timestamp, so new observations automatically invalidate the cache. NaN positions produced by the transform are reported in `metadata.gaps`. Decimal DB values are returned as float64.

422.description: Invalid TransformSpec: unknown op or invalid params. See spec for valid op values.

get.description: Returns scheduled and realized release events for economic indicators. Filter by `month` (YYYY-MM format) and/or `category` (pt-BR category name). Daily-frequency series are excluded from the calendar per FR-6.7. Events with scheduled_for in the past will have status='realized'; future events will have status='expected'.

parameters.name: month

schema.description: Filter by calendar month in YYYY-MM format (e.g. '2026-05').
schema.title: Month

parameters.description: Filter by calendar month in YYYY-MM format (e.g. '2026-05').

get.description: Returns the current user preferences including pinned series, per-card transform specs, and recently viewed series codes. In single-user v1, always returns the default user row (id=1). Returns an empty state (no pins, no transforms, empty recents) on first call.

patch.description:
Partial update of user preferences. All body fields are optional — omitting a field leaves it unchanged.

**Pin management:** Use `add_pins` and `remove_pins` lists. add_pins is idempotent (already-pinned series are ignored). Removing a pin also removes its card_transform.

**card_transforms:** Map of series_code → TransformSpec dict. Set value to null to remove the transform and revert to 'level'. Only listed series_codes are updated.

**recents:** Full replacement list (max 3 entries kept).

Returns the complete updated UserPrefsRead after applying all changes.

404.description: One or more series codes in add_pins not found.

post.summary: Manually trigger extraction for a series
post.description: Triggers an immediate extraction run for the given series code. Fetches new observations from the configured upstream source (BCB SGS, IBGE SIDRA, or Yahoo Finance/B3), upserts them into the database, and updates series.status and last_success_at. Incremental: fetches since last_success_at if set, else full backfill. Returns outcome with count of inserted/updated observations. Note: No authentication required in v1 (single-user local deployment). LIVE NETWORK CALL — runs against real upstream APIs.

200.description: Extraction completed (check status field for success/failure).

422.description: Series source cannot be mapped to a known adapter.

series_code.title: Series Code
series_code.description: Series code this transform applies to.

CardTransformRead.title: CardTransformRead
CardTransformRead.description: Active transform spec for a pinned Painel card.

status.title: Status
status.description: Outcome: 'success' | 'failed'. Mirrors series.status after the run.

observations_upserted.title: Observations Upserted
observations_upserted.description: Number of observation rows inserted or updated.

latest_observed_at.title: Latest Observed At
latest_observed_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent observation after this extraction. Null on failure.

extraction_at.title: Extraction At
extraction_at.description: UTC timestamp when the extraction was triggered.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message if the extraction failed. Null on success.

ExtractionResultResponse.title: ExtractionResultResponse
ExtractionResultResponse.description:
Response schema for POST /admin/extract/{code}.

Reports the outcome of a manual extraction trigger.

date.title: Date
date.description: ISO-8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD) of the gap.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Reason for the gap. Currently always 'missing_upstream'.

GapRecord.title: GapRecord
GapRecord.description: A single NaN gap detected in a transformed series.

status.title: Status
status.description: Aggregate status: 'ok' if all series are fresh, 'degraded' otherwise.

series.title: Series
series.description: Per-series freshness list ordered by category then code.

checked_at.title: Checked At
checked_at.description: UTC timestamp when this health check was computed.

HealthResponse.title: HealthResponse
HealthResponse.description:
Response schema for GET /health.

Summarises overall system health and per-series freshness.

series_code.title: Series Code
series_code.description: Series code these observations belong to.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total rows matching the query before limit was applied.

returned.title: Returned
returned.description: Number of rows in this response.

observed_at.title: Observed At
observed_at.description: UTC timestamp of the observation (timezone-aware ISO-8601).

value.title: Value
value.description: Numeric value as float64. Source precision: Numeric(20, 6).

ingested_at.title: Ingested At
ingested_at.description: UTC timestamp when this row was first written to the database.

ObservationRead.title: ObservationRead
ObservationRead.description:
A single time-series data point.

Note: `value` is returned as float (converted from Decimal at the DB
boundary). Precision is preserved to 6 decimal places by the DB schema.

items.title: Items
items.description: Release events ordered by scheduled_for ascending.

month.title: Month
month.description: Month filter applied, if any (YYYY-MM format).

series_code.title: Series Code
series_code.description: Series code this release belongs to.

ReleaseRead.title: ReleaseRead
ReleaseRead.description:
A scheduled or realized release event for an economic indicator.

Daily-frequency series are excluded from releases (FR-6.7).

last_success_at.title: Last Success At
last_success_at.description: UTC timestamp of last successful extraction. Null if never extracted.

SeriesFreshness.title: SeriesFreshness
SeriesFreshness.description: Freshness record for a single series, returned inside HealthResponse.

items.title: Items
items.description: List of all series ordered by category then code.

source.title: Source
source.description: Data source name, e.g. 'BCB SGS', 'IBGE SIDRA'.

source_id.title: Source Id
source_id.description: Source-system identifier, e.g. '433' for BCB SGS.

first_observation.title: First Observation
first_observation.description: Date of the earliest known observation for this series.

last_extraction_at.title: Last Extraction At
last_extraction_at.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent extraction attempt.

last_success_at.title: Last Success At
last_success_at.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent successful extraction.

SeriesRead.title: SeriesRead
SeriesRead.description:
Full metadata for a single economic indicator series.

Numeric values stored as Decimal in Postgres are returned as float here.

gaps.title: Gaps
gaps.description: List of NaN positions detected after transform. Empty when no gaps.

stub.title: Stub
stub.description: True when the op is a stub (sa, calendar_adj) — values are level passthrough.

cached.title: Cached
cached.description: True if the result was served from Redis cache.

latest_observed_at.title: Latest Observed At
latest_observed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent observation used for cache keying.

TransformMetadata.title: TransformMetadata
TransformMetadata.description: Metadata about a transform computation result.

op.title: Op
op.description: Transform operation identifier. One of: level, sa, calendar_adj, mom, qoq, yoy, annualized, diff, log_diff, pp, ma, ewma, accum12, stddev12, rebase, zscore, percentile.

params.title: Params
params.description: Operation parameters. op-specific keys: ma → {window: int (default 12)}, ewma → {span: int (default 12)}, rebase → {base: float (default 100.0)}. All other ops ignore params.

series_code.title: Series Code
series_code.description: Series code the transform was applied to.

values.title: Values
values.description: Transformed time series ordered by date ascending.

value.title: Value
value.description: Transformed float value. null where the transform produced NaN.

TransformValuePoint.title: TransformValuePoint
TransformValuePoint.description: A single (date, value) point in a transform result.

pins.title: Pins
pins.description: Pinned series ordered by display order.

card_transforms.title: Card Transforms
card_transforms.description: Active transform spec per pinned card. Absent entries default to 'level'.

recents.title: Recents
recents.description: Recently viewed series codes, most recent first (max 3).

UserPrefsRead.title: UserPrefsRead
UserPrefsRead.description:
Full user preferences response.

Represents the single-user state including pinned series,
per-card transform specs, and recently viewed series codes.

add_pins.title: Add Pins
add_pins.description: Series codes to add to pins (idempotent — already-pinned codes are ignored).

remove_pins.title: Remove Pins
remove_pins.description: Series codes to remove from pins. Unpinning also removes the card transform.

card_transforms.title: Card Transforms
card_transforms.description: Map of series_code → TransformSpec dict (or null to remove). Only listed series are updated; others are unchanged.

recents.title: Recents
recents.description: Full replacement of the recents list (max 3 entries kept).

UserPrefsUpdate.title: UserPrefsUpdate
UserPrefsUpdate.description:
Partial update body for PATCH /user_prefs.

All fields are optional — unset fields are unchanged.

Pins use add/remove lists rather than full replacement to prevent
accidental data loss on concurrent edits.

card_transforms is a mapping from series_code to TransformSpec dict
(or null to remove the transform and revert to level).

tags.name: health
tags.description: System health and per-series freshness status.

tags.name: series
tags.description: Economic indicator series metadata, observations, and raw data access. 25 Brazilian macro indicators: IPCA, SELIC, CDI, PIB, Ibovespa, etc.

tags.name: transform
tags.description: On-demand statistical transforms computed server-side via pandas. Supports 17 ops: level, MoM, YoY, MA, EWMA, z-score, rebase, and more. Results cached in Redis (TTL per series frequency).

tags.name: releases
tags.description: Economic indicator release calendar. Upcoming (E) and realized (R) events from IBGE, BCB, and hardcoded schedules.

tags.name: user_prefs
tags.description: User preferences: pinned series for Painel, per-card transform specs, and recently viewed series. Persisted in Postgres (single-user, id=1).

tags.name: admin
tags.description: Administrative operations. Manual extraction trigger (live network call). No authentication required in v1 single-user deployment.
